title: "Accidental Education" Reality Lab
author: Talk Red
description: Accidental Education: Reality Lab is a curiosity-driven podcast where odd facts, fascinating stories, and surprising slices of history collide. Each episode starts with a simple question and spirals into unexpected discoveries—proving that the best learning often happens by accident. Come for the weird, stay for the “wait, that’s real?” moments.

Your Local Plumbing Experts Ready When You Need Us

When you need a dependable Plumber in North Wales, trust the professionals at On Point Plumbing & Heating. We provide comprehensive plumbing solutions for homeowners and businesses throughout the area, delivering quality workmanship, prompt service, and lasting results. Whether you’re facing a plumbing emergency, dealing with clogged drains, or planning a system upgrade, our experienced team is here to help.
At On Point Plumbing & Heating, we understand how important a properly functioning plumbing system is to your comfort and daily routine. That’s why we are committed to providing reliable services, transparent communication, and exceptional customer care on every project. Call us today at (267) 638-7151 to schedule service with a trusted local plumber.

title: Rewinding Success
author: Reva Desai
description: Success can look obvious from the outside — but the real story starts much earlier. Rewinding Success with Reva Desai goes back to the early choices, risks, failures, mentors, and turning points that shaped leaders, builders, and changemakers into who they are today. Each conversation uncovers lessons young people can actually use as they build their own path.

description:
A father-son podcast from Colonial Painting Pros in Naples, Florida.
I sit down with my dad, JR, to talk through painting, business, family, and the lessons he’s learned from decades in the trade. Some episodes will be about painting tips and homeowner questions, and others will be more about the stories, mistakes, and experiences that shaped our business.

title: WhenJillianSpeaks
author: Jillian Lee
description: Welcome to WhenJillianSpeaks — a podcast hosted by Jillian Lee where real conversations about love, healing, self-worth, heartbreak, growth, and emotional struggles come to life. Through personal stories and honest reflections, Jillian creates a safe space for anyone feeling lost, unheard, or emotionally exhausted. Each episode is a reminder that healing takes time, your feelings are valid, and you deserve the space to grow, love yourself, and start again.

title: The 10-Minute Advantage
author: Robert Coles
description: Business growth doesn’t have to be complicated—or time-consuming. Join Robert Coles each week for practical 10-minute lessons on marketing, AI, operations, sales, productivity, and business strategy that you can put into action immediately. One idea. Ten minutes. Better business.

title: Dinner at the Rectory
author: dinnerattherectory
description: Join Fr. Mike and Fr. Tim for a monthly conversation around the table on what they have been reading and reflecting upon over the past month.

title: Founder Stories
author: Tech Oman
description: Founder Stories is the flagship podcast of the Tech Oman Innovation Campus, Oman's community-led home for founders, mentors, and investors. Every episode sits down with an entrepreneur from Oman and the wider GCC to trace the real arc of building a company — the origin story, the moment they leapt, the technology and decisions that set them apart, and the lessons earned along the way. From traditional businesses reinvented through tech to first-time founders scaling from zero, these are candid, practical conversations, not highlight reels. Whether you're building, mentoring, investing, or just

title: Beyond The Recitation
author: Nasir
description:
Beyond the Recitation is a podcast for those who want a deeper relationship with the Quran — not just to recite it, but to truly understand it.

Each episode is a conversation with someone who carries the Quran close — exploring how they connect with the words of Allah beyond memorisation. 


Conversations with those who carry the Quran.

title: StandUp Wireless
author: StandUp Wireless
description: StandUp Wireless provides the StandUp Wireless free government phone service through the Lifeline Program, helping eligible low-income individuals stay connected with free monthly phone service. Qualified customers may receive free talk, text, and data with no monthly bills or long-term contracts. In addition to Lifeline benefits, StandUp Wireless also offers affordable prepaid plans starting at just $5 per month for customers who need extra flexibility. Check your eligibility online, apply in minutes, manage your account, recertify your Lifeline benefits, or add minutes and data with ease. Stay connected with reliable wireless service designed to keep you in touch with family, work, healthcare, and emergency services.

title: Boardroom Ledger
author: 3Peaks
description: Boardroom Ledger is for operators who've just become their company's most critical finance voice—whether the CFO title exists yet or not. We unpack the strategic finance decisions that actually move the needle: what boards hunt for in your numbers, building a finance function on a shoestring, spotting the signals that mean exit planning starts now. Real founders and operators share the wins and the wreckage, because the finance seat is where strategy meets survival.

title: Woningontruiming Zoetermeer
author: Daan De Vries
description: Welkom bij de podcast van Woningontruiming Zoetermeer, dé plek waar we praktische informatie, tips en inzichten delen over professionele woningontruiming. Of het nu gaat om een verhuizing, een nalatenschap, een seniorenverhuizing of het leegmaken van een woning na een bijzondere situatie, wij bespreken hoe een woningontruiming efficiënt, respectvol en zorgvuldig kan worden uitgevoerd. Onze afleveringen behandelen veelvoorkomende vragen, handige voorbereidingen en waardevolle adviezen om het proces zo soepel mogelijk te laten verlopen. Daarnaast delen we ervaringen uit de praktijk en leggen we uit waar je op kunt letten bij het kiezen van een betrouwbare specialist. Met onze kennis helpen we particulieren, families en bedrijven om goed geïnformeerde keuzes te maken. Bij Woningontruiming Zoetermeer staan kwaliteit, betrouwbaarheid en persoonlijke service centraal. Bezoek onze website https://woningontruimingzoetermeer.com voor meer informatie over onze dienstverlening, praktische tips en het aanvragen van een vrijblijvende offerte. Luister mee en ontdek hoe een professionele woningontruiming rust, overzicht en gemak kan bieden in iedere situatie.

title: Built For This with Talia Bronfman and Jessica Green
author: Talia Bronfman and Jessica Green
description:
Stop waiting for the blueprint. Start building a life that actually fits you.
Built For This is a Canadian podcast about the business of womanhood—ambition, caregiving, relationships, money, identity, reinvention, and the invisible work shaping women’s lives. Hosted by Jessica Green and Talia Bronfman, each episode features honest conversations with women who have built something real: a company, career, family, community, second act, or new version of themselves. Smart, emotionally honest conversations women actually see themselves in.
Welcome to Built For This.
Instagram: @built_for_this.

title: The Friendly Behavour
author: hanli puala
description: Welcome to Things on My Mind, the podcast where honest thoughts become meaningful conversations. Every episode explores the ideas, questions, and observations that shape the way we think, live, and grow. From personal development and relationships to success, faith, mental resilience, current issues, and everyday life lessons, nothing is off-limits.This is a space for reflection, curiosity, and real talk—where complex topics are broken down into practical insights that inspire positive change. Whether you're seeking motivation, fresh perspectives, or simply a thoughtful conversation to accompany your day, you'll find something here that resonates.Join us as we explore the thoughts that challenge assumptions, spark meaningful discussions, and encourage intentional living. Sometimes the smallest idea can lead to the biggest

title: The Enchanted Mouse Ears Podcast
author: Shawn DeVore
description: Welcome to The Enchanted Mouse Ears Podcast, where we celebrate the history, stories, and magic of Walt Disney World. Join your host, Shawn DeVore, as we explore favorite attractions, Disney history, Imagineering, park updates, and the little details that make the parks special. From beloved classics to new experiences, we’ll share stories, discuss what makes these places memorable, and look at where Disney has been, where it is today, and where it’s headed tomorrow. Come join our Disney family and discover the magic behind the magic.

title: Our Story
author: College of the Ozarks
description:
From passionate professors to first generation college students, each one has left what is easy and known to reach for something more. Their stories are inspiring. Some of them will bring you to your knees, some will make you stand and cheer. Step into the shoes of the cycle-breakers… You won’t regret it, and you just may come away inspired.
Listen in each month as we hear the stories of the cycle-breakers, passion seekers, and image bearers who write the College of the Ozarks story, one chapter at a time.

title: Your Questions Answered
author: Police & Crime Commissioner
description:
What happens to the questions you raise about policing in South Wales?

Every day, South Wales Police & Crime Commissioner Emma Wools hears directly from residents about the issues affecting their communities, from anti-social behaviour to police visibility and public safety. 

Your Questions Answered puts those concerns at the centre of the conversation. From crime prevention and public safety to police performance and accountability, each episode explores the issues that matter most to local people.

Join the Police & Crime Commissioner as she puts your questions directly to the Chief Constable for South Wales Police. Together they discuss the issues impacting communities, the action being taken to tackle them, and the challenges facing modern policing.

author: Noah Mearns
description: The greatness of the world's game

title: The Kid Who... | True Stories of Real Kid Heroes
author: Jon Michael
description:
The Kid Who... is a kids podcast that tells incredible true stories of real kids who changed history, showed extraordinary courage, solved impossible problems, and proved you're never too young to make a difference.
Each family-friendly episode is filled with suspense, surprising twists, and unforgettable heroes, all based on real events. From brave kids who saved lives to young inventors, explorers, athletes, and everyday children who did the extraordinary, every story inspires curiosity, confidence, resilience, and kindness.
Perfect for car rides, road trips, bedtime, classrooms, homeschooling, and screen free family time, The Kid Who... gives kids ages 5 to 12 exciting stories they'll actually ask to hear again, and gives parents educational entertainment they can feel great about.
If your family loves true stories for kids, educational podcasts, inspiring real life adventures, history for kids, and podcasts the whole family can enjoy together, you've found your next favorite listen.
Because sometimes...the biggest heroes are...little.

title: (So) How's Business?
author: alma mabachi
description:
(So) How's Business? is the podcast for African women entrepreneurs who are figuring it out as they go. Hosted by Alma Mabachi, a bootstrapping founder and mother, this show trades polished success stories for raw, honest conversations.
Every week is a candid look into the lives of women entrepreneurs and hustlers navigating the messy middle: the pivots, failures, small wins, big dreams, and what it actually takes to build something from scratch. 
Connect with us @sohowsbusiness on IG, X, Facebook, Tiktok, and Linkedin. Send us an email on sohowsbusinesspodcast@gmail.com

title: Journey Through LBS
author: Robert Hickman
description:
Journey Through LBS is a podcast dedicated to sharing authentic stories of perseverance, transformation, and purpose. Each episode features athletes, musicians, entrepreneurs, creators, and everyday people who have overcome obstacles and embraced the journey that shaped their lives.
In addition to these inspiring life stories, the podcast shines a light on incredible health and weight-loss journeys, exploring the physical, mental, and emotional challenges behind lasting change. Guests share their struggles, victories, setbacks, and the lessons they've learned along the way, offering encouragement and practical insight for anyone striving to become a better version of themselves.

title: STILL HERE: The Car-Living Tapes
author: PRASAD
description: An audio diary recorded inside the Toyota Corolla where Himanshu Prasad currently lives in Los Angeles. These are not stories told after the struggle has ended. They are reflections from inside it, on ambition, survival, discipline, faith, loneliness, identity, and continuing without knowing how the story will turn out. An unfinished life, documented in real time.

description: A free monthly webinar series designed to help primary producers navigate the realities of running their farming business. Featuring Rural Financial Counsellors and industry experts, each session tackles the topics shaping life on the land.

With 14+ years of industry experience and over 1,200 successful projects delivered, Talentelgia shares expert insights, practical strategies, and real-world success stories to help startups, enterprises, and tech enthusiasts innovate, scale, and stay ahead in the digital world. Tune in for actionable discussions on AI, automation, product development, and business growth.

description: Want to know what’s really happening in the Lehigh Valley? Voice of the Valley features exclusive interviews with local business owners, nonprofit directors, athletes, and community leaders who are making a positive impact. Hosted by Erin and the crew, we explore the vibrant culture, rich history, and future of our region. Follow along for your weekly dose of inspiration from the heart of PA!

title: Gugu – The Parents' Guide

title: You Are Enough With Michelle Jacob
author: Michelle Jacob
description: You Are Enough with Michelle Jacob is a transformational podcast for anyone ready to deepen their mental, emotional, and spiritual wellness. Hosted by Michelle Jacob, Emotional Wellness Expert, award-winning author, founder of Rewiring Minds™, and creator of the Quantum Inner Transformation Technique™, each episode explores how childhood wounds, subconscious beliefs, and nervous system patterns shape identity, relationships, leadership, parenting, wealth, and self-worth — helping you uncover what keeps you stuck and step into who you were always meant to become.

title: Fuel the Change: Midlife Nutrition Reset
author: Juliana Vocca
description:
Your body is changing — and your nutrition strategy needs to change with it.

Registered Dietitian Juliana Vocca, MS, RD brings 20+ years of clinical expertise to the conversations women in their 40s, 50s, and beyond actually need to have — from the metabolic shifts of perimenopause to the nutritional demands of GLP-1 medications.

Featured in Fortune, Prevention, Eating Well, and Real Simple, Juliana cuts through the noise with real-world strategies that work for real women's lives. Every episode delivers science-backed guidance you can act on today.

title: Honestly Kristin
author: Kristin Palm Kane
description: the Honestly Kristin Podcast, the honest, sometimes ridiculous business of being a woman in this stretch of life. We discuss the things that ache, the stuff that is just absurd, and the friends that join us along the way.

title: Peace Corps Sri Lanka Podcast

description: The Peace Corps Sri Lanka Podcast brings you stories from Volunteers serving in rural villages across the island — the challenges, the small victories, and the everyday moments that make service unforgettable.

Dear Lovies, 
Welcome to Love Always, Myles is where healing meets honesty.
A soft, intentional space for reflection, growth, and becoming. Each episode feels like a letter written for the parts of you still figuring it out. About love, attachment, letting go, and choosing yourself again and again.
Come as you are and leave a light lighter
Love Always, Myles
Connect us

title: Mission Voices | Conversations Shaping Humanity's Future in Space
author: Mars Initiative
description:
*Every journey to Mars begins with a story.*
Mission Voices is The Mars Initiative's original interview series and podcast exploring the people shaping humanity's future in space.
Hosted by *Lexie Garrett* , each episode features conversations with astronauts, engineers, scientists, educators, advocates, entrepreneurs, and innovators working to advance the next era of human exploration. Together, we explore the stories behind the missions, the breakthroughs behind the headlines, and the ideas driving humanity's return to the Moon, journey to Mars, and future beyond Earth.
Whether you're a lifelong space enthusiast, a student dreaming of a career in STEM, or simply curious about where humanity is headed next, Mission Voices invites you to discover the voices inspiring tomorrow's explorers.
🚀 Learn more about The Mars Initiative: ** *https://marsinitiative.org* ( https://marsinitiative.org/ ) *﻿*
*Because every journey to Mars begins with a story.* 🌎➡️🔴

title: Queer Kinship : a show about friends
author: Skip Loehr
description:
Queer Kinship : a show about friends is a podcast exploring chosen family, the attachment dynamics of friendship, relational healing, friendship as activism, distributed co-regulation, and the many ways queer // queer-allied communities create, repair, navigate, and nurture belonging together. 
Skip is a licensed mental health counselor currently living on unceded Tewa land in Santa Fe, New Mexico. 
----
The conversations on this podcast are educational and reflective in nature. They are not a substitute for psychotherapy, medical care, or individualized mental health treatment. The views expressed by guests are their own and do not necessarily reflect the views of the host. Some episodes include discussions of grief, trauma, mental health, discrimination, conflict, and other emotionally difficult experiences.

The two flops Molly and Paige discuss pop culture, personal lives, and the flops of the week

title: Prisoners that change the world
author: Frank Stedman
description:
Prisoners Who Changed the World

Presented by Frank Stedman


What happens when someone is locked away from the world… and comes out having changed it?


In this podcast, Frank Stedman explores the lives of some of history’s most influential figures who spent time in prison — not just who they were, but how prison shaped their thinking, their mindset, and ultimately their impact on the world.


From political leaders to writers, revolutionaries to thinkers, each episode looks beyond the headlines to understand what prison does to a person — and how, in some cases, it can transform them.


This isn’t about glorifying crime.

It’s about understanding change, resilience, and the human mind under pressure.

title: Faith Forward - The Refuge, Spangdahlem
author: Gary Stalnaker
description:
Faith Forward is a podcast from The Refuge in Spangdahlem, Germany. A Freedom Outreach ministry to the military community.
Each episode equips believers through the teaching of God’s Word, strengthens and encourages faith, and calls listeners to go deeper in their walk with the Lord.
Wherever you are in your walk today, the goal is the same: take the next step forward, grounded in Scripture and living on mission.

title: Say What?
author: Claire
description:
History is full of stories. Language is full of mysteries.
Every week, Say What? investigates the curious origins of the words, phrases, names and legends we use without a second thought. From Peeping Tom to Bob’s your uncle and everything in between, each short case file follows the evidence to uncover what really happened in less than fifteen minutes.

title: Lions 105CE Talking Newsletter
author: Lions 105CE
description: An audio edition of the Lions 105CE Newsletter

title: INT Institute Podcast
author: INT Institute
description: Learn, grow, and stay future-ready with the INT Institute Podcast, featuring expert discussions on IT, technology, career opportunities, and skill development.

title: Dad the Influencer — a podcast about travel, technology and innovation
author: Dad the Influencer
description:
Dad the Influencer — a podcast about travel, technology and innovation broad enough to encompass history, art, religion, and politics in ways that will stimulate the neurodivergent and amaze the neurotypical. With interviews, dialogues and the occasional monologue, we explore our humanity through travel, the technology that enables it, and ecosystems that encourage elastic thinking and innovation.
**The opinions expressed in this podcast are my own and do not necessarily reflect those of my employers**

title: Digital Dust: The Man Will Talk
author: BurnerSphere
description: The virtual Burning Man world featured in HBO's The Man Will Burn now has its own podcast about the doc. Four burners gather around a table inside BurnerSphere (BRCvr presents BurnerSphere) — the VR playa where doc star Lindsay Handren's Burning Man story began — to break down the series episode by episode: the culture, the history, and what HBO got right (and wrong) about the burn. We don't speak for HBO or the Burning Man Project — just burners with opinions.

title: Moving Beyond Dispute
author: Mark Sinclair
description: The podcast that takes you inside the world of mediation and conflict resolution. We speak with leading mediators, conflict coaches, psychologists, researchers and industry experts to unpack the complexity and nature of disputes, look at what actually works when stakes are high and tensions run deep, understand what it’s like for the mediator, and to learn more about how people react when you bring them face to face to find common ground.

title: Physical Film Files
author: Flix Forum
description:
A monthly podcast where one film from a physical media collection is pulled from the shelf to take a closer look at what makes it worth shelf space.
From the film itself, to the artwork, the packaging, and the special features—this is about celebrating movies the physical way.
If you’re a collector, a film fan, or someone who still loves opening a case and pressing play, this show is for you.

title: The NextXI Cricket Show
author: NextXI Cricket
description:
The NEXTXI's Cricket Podcast podcast covering the stories shaping Australian state cricket. 
From the Sheffield Shield and One Day Cup to the WNCL and T20 Spring Challenge, we go beyond the scorecard with player interviews, match analysis, selection debates, state rivalries and the moments that matter. 
Featuring current and former players, coaches and special guests, the NextXI Cricket Podcast is where the conversation continues after the final ball. 
NEXTXI: Where Australian cricket takes shape.

title: Psychic Raj Guru
author: Psychic Raj Guru
description: Psychic Raj Guru has more than 25 years of experience guiding people through love, relationships, and life challenges. As a specialist in love spells in Portland, he offers personalized spiritual guidance, love spell solutions, relationship healing, and accurate psychic insights. His empathetic approach and years of experience have helped countless people regain happiness, strengthen their relationships, and find peace of mind.

title: Things on my mind
author: stephen mollower
description: 'Things on My Mind' introduces personal reflections, thoughts, and insights, setting the stage for an introspective series that explores everyday concerns and musings.

title: Refining Fire Podcast
author: I am Matthew Ministries
description:
Four guys. One crucible. No script.
What happens when you strip away the polished sermons and the rehearsed talking points? You get Refining Fire, a podcast where faith meets the heat of the moment. In every episode, four friends sit around the table with no idea what’s coming. The topic of the day stays locked away until it’s pulled live from the Crucible. What follows is raw, unscripted, and honest conversation about the Bible, life, and what it actually looks like to walk out your faith in real-time. We aren't here to give you the "perfect" answer—we’re here to work through it together!

title: The YTH Culture Podcast
author: Rony Galdamez
description:
Hosted by Rony Galdamez, The YTH Culture Podcast is a youth culture show produced by Pio Youth, the youth movement of the Shrine of St. Padre Pio Catholic Church in San Antonio.
This podcast highlights real stories from within our community and beyond it, with testimonies of how God is moving, conversations about culture, sports, and what’s happening in the world today, all through the lens of faith.
Each episode features honest conversations with guests, practical insights, and moments that point to God working in everyday life.
Faith, culture, and real stories.

title: Next Unicorn: The Next Great Tech Startups & Founders
author: Sasha Earnheart-Gold
description: Searching for the next great tech startups, we interview startup founders, venture capital investors, growth hackers, product specialists, technologists, legal experts, and others. Website: https://www.nextunicornpodcast.com/

title: This Is Lucy Podcast
author: Lucy Shahjahan
description:
This Is Lucy Podcast is a storytelling podcast about what happens when you stop betraying yourself, take the lid off who you really are, and start saying yes to the life that is calling you.
Through hilarious, vulnerable, and sometimes completely unbelievable stories from her own life, Lucy shares the wake-up calls, sliding-door moments, manifestations, romantic disasters, bold decisions, and divine interventions that helped her become the unapologetic woman she is today.
Expect conversations about love, self-trust, relationships, manifestation, healing, purpose, and following your heart even when it makes absolutely no sense on paper. 
This is not a podcast about pretending life is perfect or whispering affirmations while doing nothing. It is about doing the inner work, taking the risk, raising your standards, and becoming the version of you who is ready to receive what she actually desires.
Funny, fiery, honest, and deeply personal, This is Lucy Podcast is your weekly permission slip to stop playing small, trust your own wisdom, and go all in on your life.

title: Philosophy's Not Boring
author: Kidopoly
description:
A groundbreaking podcast for curious kids aged 4-12, and the grown-ups listening with them, that proves philosophy, psychology and religion are anything but dull.

Join our fictional AI hosts Mira, a brilliant 9-year-old, and her younger brother Finn, age 7, as they explore philosophy, psychology and religion as one big family of questions about how we make sense of things. Each episode takes a real thinker or idea seriously, from what makes you the same person you were as a baby to why people across the world believe such different things about the soul, and then lands it somewhere in your actual life.

We don't hand you the answer and move on. Some questions have one; some have several worth comparing; some nobody has ever settled, and we'll tell you which is which. On matters of faith we stay even-handed: we describe what people believe from the outside, and never tell you what to believe yourself. Because philosophy isn't a pile of hard words to memorize. It's learning to ask a better question, and to sit with it honestly, even when it's uncomfortable.

A note on why we use AI. For us, AI allows us to deliver learning at a scale and quality that previously would have been too expensive. If we make the odd technical error, or the sound goes a bit funny, bear with us, we're trying our best. We hope you enjoy the show!

title: Perishable Crowns
author: Dez Ouzts & Noah Szilagyi
description:
Perishable Crowns is a sports podcast that examines athletics through a biblical worldview. We celebrate excellence, discipline, competition, and achievement while remembering that no championship, trophy, contract, or legacy lasts forever. Sports are wonderful gifts from God, but they make terrible gods.

Each week we discuss the biggest stories in sports, especially basketball, while exploring the character, integrity, virtues, failures, and worldviews that shape athletes, coaches, and organizations. We seek to appreciate greatness without idolizing it and critique failures without forgetting our shared human condition.

Our goal is to provide thoughtful sports analysis, fresh perspectives, and biblical wisdom in a media environment often dominated by outrage, tribalism, and celebrity worship.

title: SkyPlotter
author: Victoria Amelia Langley
description:
SkyPlotter is a deeply fascinating
scientific, and sensory-rich journey into the history of stellar mapping, astronomical technology, and the lost art of finding our way. If you are ready to quiet the digital noise, lift your eyes back to the night sky, and learn how to map your own path using the timeless laws of the universe.

title: Felt
author: Dana Biechonski Fernandes
description: Felt is a podcast about the body, emotions, healing, and the deeper intelligence moving through human experience. Hosted by Dana Biechonski Fernandes, somatic psychotherapist and educator, it explores embodiment, trauma, symptoms, relationships, and the wisdom of the body. Drawing from science, clinical practice, and lived experience, Felt explores what becomes possible when we slow down enough to truly listen. Dana translates complex ideas into conversations that are both grounded and deeply human—inviting a deeper relationship with ourselves, with others, and with life itself.

title: No Boss Required
author: Grant Reighard
description: Building a life without a boss one project, one decision, and one shipped product at a time.

title: The Technology Principal
author: Franklin Fitch
description: Dive into unfiltered conversations with the world’s top C‑level tech leaders as they share the bold moves, pivotal moments, and breakthrough ideas that shaped their journey. Each episode uncovers the challenges driving innovation today and the visions set to redefine tomorrow’s tech landscape. Inspiring, insightful, and packed with real leadership wisdom - this is where the future of technology gets personal.

title: Call Me Collect
author: Whuz & Earth1
description: Call Me Collect is the podcast where collectibles are just the beginning. Whether it's sports cards, Pokémon, comics, action figures, Bearbricks, Funko, vintage toys, or the latest hobby news and Pop Culture

title: Paradise Falls Podcast
author: Dear M D
description: Paradise Falls Podcast brings you immersive audiobooks and inspiring stories designed to entertain, uplift, and spark your imagination. Tune in and discover a world of meaningful storytelling—one episode at a time.

title: Hidden Stories of Tennis
author: Ethan walker
description:
Tennis is more than trophies, rankings, and championship points—it's a sport built on innovation, resilience, strategy, and unforgettable moments that often go unnoticed. Beyond the Baseline: Hidden Stories of Tennis takes you beyond the match highlights to uncover the fascinating stories, groundbreaking tactics, scientific discoveries, historic rivalries, and untold journeys that have shaped the game for generations.

Each episode blends documentary-style storytelling with expert insights, exploring everything from the evolution of the tennis racket and the psychology of elite performance to iconic tournaments, legendary champions, rising stars, and the hidden details that casual fans rarely hear. Whether we're revisiting pivotal moments in tennis history or explaining the biomechanics behind the perfect serve, every episode is designed to educate, entertain, and inspire.

title: Succesvol Transformeren Podcast
author: Transformation Tribes Highberg
description: In deze podcastreeks gaan consultants van Highberg met elkaar én met gastsprekers in gesprek over het belang van besturing en over succes- en faalfactorenten tijde van een Digitale Transformatie. In de snel veranderende digitale wereld van vandaag is het voor bedrijven en organisaties essentieel om zich aan te passen en te evolueren. Het is daarbij belangrijk om de juiste koers te bepalen en te bewaken voor en tijdens de veranderingen.

title: Beautifully Messed Up
author: Raven and Amethyst
description: A dark unfiltered podcast where chaos meets curiosity. Hosted with sharp wit and a touch of wicked humor, it dives into the raw, uncomfortable and often taboo corners of life - stories of secrets, survival, and relationships, and everything in between. It is a space where nothing is sugarcoated, vulnerability is power and the mess is what makes it beautiful.

title: Creative Juices Podcast
author: Breakfast of Champions
description:
Creative Juices Podcast is where creativity, branding, and sports collide.Hosted by Keegan Hartman, Assistant Director of Graphic Design at West Virginia Athletics, the Creative Juices Podcast takes listeners behind the scenes of the collegiate creative industry through conversations with some of the most talented designers, photographers, videographers, creative directors, and digital storytellers in sports.

Each episode explores the creative process, career journeys, branding philosophies, recruiting strategies, NIL, social media, content creation, and the ever-evolving landscape of athletics and visual storytelling. Whether you're a student looking to break into the industry, a seasoned creative searching for inspiration, or simply someone fascinated by the work that powers today's biggest athletic brands, this podcast offers authentic conversations and valuable insights from the people shaping the future of sports creative.

New episodes feature industry professionals from every level of athletics, sharing the successes, challenges, and lessons that have defined their careers.

Subscribe now and join the conversation as we celebrate the creators behind the brands, the stories behind the designs, and the passion that fuels the next generation of sports creatives.

Creative Juices Podcast — By Creatives, For Creatives.

title: Italy Salary Simplified
author: Micheal Jack
description: Every paycheck tells a story, but understanding it can be challenging. Italy Salary Simplified explains how salaries work in Italy, covering topics such as gross pay, net income, payroll deductions, IRPEF, INPS contributions, and employee benefits. Each episode turns complex salary concepts into practical information that anyone can understand. Whether you're entering the workforce, changing jobs, or planning your finances, you'll find clear explanations and useful resources.

title: TheTuesdayJewTX Podcast
author: phillip goldstein
description: Welcome to the ”Tuesday Jew” podcast—your spiritual wake-up call in a world full of distractions. We dive deep into Mussar, Chassidus, and Machshava, drawing on powerful teachings from Navardok, Slobodka, Telshe, Berdichev, Chabad, Breslov, Belz, Reb Elimelech, and Rav Kook. Tune in for a heimish perspective on navigating the madness of Ikvesa D’Meshicha and rising above the noise of modern technology.

title: Soul Sessions: Motivation for Introverts and People Pleasers
author: Benjamin The Great
description:
Soul Sessions with Benjamin The Great is a podcast dedicated to real conversations that inspire, encourage, and uplift. Each episode explores life, purpose, faith, relationships, personal growth, music, and culture with honesty and heart. Join Benjamin The Great for meaningful discussions, inspiring stories, and practical wisdom that speaks to the soul and empowers you to live with purpose. Each episode is a reminder that your quiet nature is not a weakness—it’s a strength. You don’t have to change who you are to succeed, make an impact, or live with purpose.
Whether you’re an introvert, a recovering people pleaser, or simply looking for motivation that speaks to the soul, this podcast is your space to grow, reflect, and become the best version of yourself.

title: The Path I Walk
author: Sydney Taylor
description:
What path are you on right now? Is it bumpy or smooth? Whichever path we walk on in our faith, God is constantly with us and he provides us with friends and family that walk the same path we do. Whether they are further ahead, behind, or right beside us. 
the.path.i.walk08@gmail.com

title: The Well-Trained Child
author: Eliza Southwell
description: I’m a Christian wife raising two boys. My strategies change. But Biblical principles don’t. So here's a picture of how children can be trained well. I tell stories from my home and cover topics like our routines, how I train my children, language I use (and avoid) and how motherhood is an opportunity to improve my character too.

title: Jeremy's Podcast
author: Jeremy
description:
Belief Beyond the Streets Podcast

Welcome to Belief Beyond the Streets, a podcast where hope rises above hardship and every story has the power to inspire change. Hosted by Jeremy Blaine, this podcast explores faith, redemption, personal growth, prison reform, second chances, and the resilience of the human spirit.

Through honest conversations with formerly incarcerated individuals, community leaders, advocates, and changemakers, we uncover practical tools for healing, accountability, leadership, and breaking generational cycles. Whether you’re seeking inspiration, navigating life’s challenges, or passionate about creating stronger communities, this podcast is for you.

Because true transformation begins with belief—and your past does not have to define your future.

title: For the Greater Good

title: Estate Expectations
author: Elisabeth Pickle
description:
Are you ready to transform your perception of Estate Planning? What if I told you, it's not just about paperwork, but a journey towards securing your legacy and turbocharging your brand? Intrigued? You should be! Say goodbye to confusion and hello to clarity with the Estate Expectations podcast. I'm Elisabeth Pickle, your guide in this adventure. With two decades as an estate planning attorney, I've demystified the process, safeguarded fortunes, and paved futures. 
Get ready to plunge into the depths of estate planning, unravel the threads of legacy creation, unravel the secrets of wealth transfer, and shield assets like never before. We're not stopping there – intellectual property protection, brand building, and much more are all on the menu.
Your assets, both tangible and intangible, deserve a fortress of protection. Let's empower your journey together. Tune in on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, or wherever podcast waves carry you.  Your wealth-securing, brand-boosting, legacy-crafting voyage starts now!

title: Condé Nast Traveller Podcast

description: Escape Routes with Condé Nast Traveller is our new podcast showcasing the magazine's most transportative travel storytelling. Let us take you to some of our favourite places, if only in your imagination, with the soul-lifting memoirs and daydream-worthy destinations of our most-loved travel stories, read aloud by the writers who penned them.

title: ASRÉ PODCAST
author: Arlington Scott
description: GOD | FAMILY | FELLOWSHIP. My life has been filled with lessons that I'm still learning from to this day. I now have blessings that God has given me to pass the lessons on to. This society has pulled us out of connection and makes us think our personal stories don't matter anymore, but that's just not true at all. Join me as I present a life of perseverance through ups and downs in my life and conversations with some very accomplished guests. This will be the realest podcast you will ever listen to because its your life reflected in others. Lets Fellowship.

author: Jules Duncan
description: Talking all things health & fitness

title: Based onna true story
author: brissa garcia
description:
Hey guys, what's up! I'm a girl from South Central LA. This podcast is the product of me finally having the balls to put myself out there. From unhealed childhood wounds, debilitating anxiety, making all my friends hate me, and an unconsensual rehab hold in Mexico, to getting healthy, speaking my truth, and learning to be a person again, this is my podcast, raw and uncut. Sometimes it might be funny; other times it may be uncomfortable to listen to. The only thing that I promise is authenticity. I've spent enough time hiding and being ashamed! Either way, I hope you join along for the ride. This goes out to anyone who might feel alone. Sometimes, even though you can't imagine a different reality, it doesn't mean there isn't one


Sincerely, Briss

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Chronicles of the Un-napped and the Walking Fed
author: Chris and Tiffany Gonzales
description:
Most people never realize that fostering is one of the most emotionally demanding and spiritually rewarding journeys you can take. Chris and Tiffany Gonzales share how they stepped into foster care—and ended up impacting over 13 lives in just two years. They reveal the raw truths behind the highs, the heartbreaks, and the miraculous moments of their foster family story.
Discover how listening to their story might reshape your understanding of true sacrifice and God's calling.

title: The Bounce Back Podcast
author: The Carruth Center at WVU
description:
The Bounce Back Podcast is a podcast for students at West Virginia University that breaks down resilience into real, usable tools. Join clinicians Laith Alabbad and Spring Lepack as we discuss building resiliency through honest conversations, campus-relevant stories, and clinician insight that doesn’t feel clinical. This podcast is presented by WVU’s Carruth Center’s Resiliency Project.

This podcast shares general mental health information, but it’s not a substitute for therapy. If you are a current WVU student interested in starting therapy, reach out to the Carruth Center for Counseling and Psychological Services at 304-293-4431 or schedule an appointment online at carruth.wvu.edu.

title: May She Reign
author: DALYA
description:
May She Reign is a podcast about the people who build lives on their own terms.

Hosted by Farida Raafat, founder of custom clothing brand DALYA, each episode explores the risks, pivots, instincts, and defining moments that shape who we become. Through candid conversations with founders, athletes, creatives, leaders, and change makers, May She Reign uncovers what it really means to trust yourself, carve your own path, and reign in your own life.

title: The invincible brain
author: Dr. Majid Fotuhi
description:
Your brain can get better at any age, and Dr. Majid Fotuhi will show you how.
A Harvard and Johns Hopkins-trained neurologist and author of the USA Today bestseller The Invincible Brain, Dr. Fotuhi turns cutting-edge neuroscience into simple steps you can act on this week.
Each episode explores the five pillars of brain health, exercise, diet, sleep, stress reduction, and brain training to help you sharpen memory, beat brain fog, and build a stronger, more resilient brain.
New episodes every week.
Take your free brain health assessment at invincible.drfotuhi.com

description: Sofia Seni is a 21 year old girl starting her spiritual/ self growth journey. She has created a modern day wellness podcast to share her learnings and help others grow with love.

This podcast will feature Elephant team members breaking down every unique challenge of small business ownership in Australia, from the good times, the bad times and the ugly times. With a little help, you'll be prepared to make the right decisions for your business and avoid making our many mistakes. So join us as we begin a podcast journey to improving your small business. www.elephantadvisory.com.au/services/accounting

title: Do It or Don’t
author: Big Time Publishing
description:
Conversations with women who finally did the thing.
Started the business. Wrote the book. Shared the art. Learned the hobby. Changed directions. Became visible. Bet on themselves.
A show about creative courage, unfinished dreams, identity, motherhood, ambition, and the moments that change everything.
Because whether you do it or don’t… time keeps passing.

title: Between The Pours
author: Dancing Water Winery
description: Between The Pours is New Zealand's rosé podcast, long-form conversations with the winemakers, viticulturists, and passionate people behind the country's finest drops. Hosted by Kim Schofield of Dancing Water in Waipara, North Canterbury, each episode goes beyond the tasting notes and into the real stories: the harvests that went wrong, the risks that paid off, and the moments that made someone choose a life in wine. New episodes every month. Subscribe and join us between the pours.

title: The Future State
author: Dr Emmanuel Ogungbemi
description: The Future State explores how technology, artificial intelligence, law, leadership, governance, and society are shaping the world of tomorrow. Hosted by Dr Emmanuel Ogungbemi, each episode helps leaders, professionals, policymakers, and curious minds understand what is changing, why it matters, and what we should do about it.

author: beardedguyonaharley
description:
Step into the unapologetic world of a Marine, biker, musician, and patriot who’s loud, proud, and unfiltered. Expect bold opinions, raw truth, and a few laughs along the way. Whether you’re here for the wisdom, the wild stories, or just to hear someone who lives life full throttle, you’re in the right place.

👉 Buckle up, ride free, and don’t take any sh*t from anyone.

description: Selah with Say is a Christ-centered space for guided Christian meditation, breathwork, prayer, mindfulness and scripture reflection. These gentle, Scripture-rooted meditations help overwhelmed Christians to slow down, breathe, receive God’s Word and rise with peace, healing and spiritual strength. Rooted in Jesus Christ, the Word of God and the presence of the Holy Spirit. Rest in God. Rise in strength.

description:
A news podcast that keeps you up to date with the latest developments in radiology and medical imaging.
From medical AI and diagnostic imaging to conferences, events, and the latest research, we break down the topics that matter in a concise and easy-to-follow format.
Designed for busy healthcare professionals, it's a convenient way to stay informed during your commute or whenever you have a few spare minutes.

title: Injecting Leadership
author: Injecting Leadership
description:
Injecting Leadership is the podcast that discusses leadership through the lens of the medical aesthetics industry.
Hosted by two seasoned leaders from the medical aesthetics industry, each show features a discussion with a special guest where they’ll explore how the precision, care, and perspective needed in medical aesthetics translate into powerful leadership lessons.
Each episode delivers inspiring stories and practical tools for motivating teams, driving innovation, and leading with love.

title: Watch Design Time
author: Sean
description: Welcome to Watch Design Time, a podcast dedicated to the art, engineering, and creative philosophies behind watch design and watchmaking. We move past the superficial noise to focus on how timepieces actually come to life. From independent microbrand creators working through physical prototyping constraints to the design teams inside historic houses like Bulgari, we explore the real stories behind the wrist. Join us as we break down the distinct cultural influences, mechanical limitations, and design choices that shape the watches we love.

title: Where You Meet Yourself
author: Liv Arcangeli
description:
Somewhere beneath the roles you play and the expectations you carry, there's a version of you waiting to be set free. Welcome to Where You Meet Yourself.
I'm Liv Arcangeli — transformational coach, energy healer, and artist. I'm here to get real with you. We strip away everything that is not you, so you can return to the core of who you truly are. Because from that place your magic lives, your boldness comes alive, and you create the work, the art, and the impact you're here to make.
This is your invitation to meet yourself, again and again, maybe for the very first time.

description: The show that asks the questions about insurance nobody else thinks to ask, then goes and finds the answer. One honest question per episode, from supertankers to the World Cup, each one revealing a corner of the world that couldn't run without insurance. Everything is insurance. Even the things that aren't.

From Nacht Der Untoten to Rex Infernus and every twisted chapter in between, no topic is off limits. Packed with passionate discussion, lethal analysis and the occasional bit of patter, this podcast is the perfect companion for slaying the undead hordes.

Welcome to Grind Theory, the podcast for anyone who loves specialty coffee or wants to learn more.
Each episode I explore a coffee from roasters around the world, covering its origin, producer, processing, brewing, tasting notes, and the story behind the cup. I’ll taste each coffee as espresso and filter, sharing honest thoughts and what I learn along the way.
Grind Theory is also about the people, the culture, and making specialty coffee welcoming and accessible. So grab a coffee, get comfortable, and let’s explore together.

Under Wraps with Jessica is a space for real stories with real people, meaningful conversations, and the journeys that shape us. From women sharing their personal experiences with head wrapping to the occasional unexpected turn, no two episodes are exactly alike.
Some stories are about what we wear and others are about who we become along the way. Either way, there’s always more to discover Under Wraps.
Hosted by Jessica Hyam from The Little Tichel Lady.

author: Podily

The Book of Psalms Podcast features peaceful Bible readings, Christian prayer, Scripture reflection, and daily devotion inspired by the Book of Psalms. Each episode shares biblical wisdom, encouragement, worship, hope, healing, and spiritual guidance to help listeners grow in faith and draw closer to God.

Listen for calming Psalm readings, Christian meditation, morning devotion, evening prayer, Bible study, and uplifting Scripture for everyday life. Whether you are seeking peace, strength, comfort, inspiration, or a deeper understanding of the Bible, The Book of Psalms Podcast offers meaningful Christian content rooted in God’s Word.

description:
Hey, everybody, I’m Anne-Marie Michaelis, a Christian Author and Coach. Welcome to the Hidden Battles Podcast! A weekly podcast about the hidden battles of spiritual warfare, the struggles in our minds and hearts, AND the truth about overcoming these “hidden battles.” My life was radically transformed when I was saved and delivered during a supernatural encounter with Jesus. God allowed my eyes to be spiritually opened long enough to SEE the truth of these hidden battles and to scare the rebellion against Him- right out of me.

Master the business of fashion with Signal Stitch.
We connect creative innovation with the realities of retail, supporting designers, label founders, retailers, and industry pros.
We dive into honest conversations exploring the journeys of UK fashion designers, brands, and retailers. 
From sustainable production to scaling retail operations, we deliver insights you need to navigate today’s fashion landscape.
Join the host and expert guests as they share practical knowledge and real-world experiences to help you build a lasting fashion business. Follow & visit signalstitch.co.uk to stay updated

title: Burnt Shores
author: Rick Devereaux | Caribbean and Sea Thrillers
description:
Cap Gilroy was just an aimless Uber driver scraping by on the Florida Gulf Coast when a marina night guard is brutally murdered on his late father’s classic boat. Hoping to just stay out of the way of the police, Cap discovers that his absentee dad wasn't just a paranoid survivalist, but a deep-cover CIA operative who hid a package before his death, a package everyone now seems desperate to find.
Now, the ultimate deadly treasure hunt is on. Pursued by cartel assassins and entangled with a lethal Mossad spy, Cap must team up with his father's formidable former CIA partner to find the hidden truth before their enemies do. 
Burnt Shores is a pulse-pounding Caribbean thriller where family legacies are forged in gunfire and the deadliest secrets are hidden at sea. Each 5-15 minute episode is a pulse-pounding sprint to a secret Cap may not want to find. 
A new chapter. Every day. For free. From Surface Tension Studio.

title: Nikki Cosma: Real Talk, No Filter
author: Nikki Cosma
description:
Unscripted & unapologetic conversations from a free-spirited, sex-positive, self-employed, festival-girlie travelling the world in the 21st century - I’m Nikki Cosma, facilitator and founder of Holofractal Life Systems & Retreats. 
No notes, no script, just what’s real. New episodes whenever I feel there’s something worth sharing with y’all. Expect my craziest story-times (I will have you cry-laughing at my best and worst moments!), reflections on emotional regulation, personal self-care philosophy, women’s health navigation, financial literacy development, living a life of flow and literally anything and everything else that pops into my cheeky lil’ brain.
Tune in, have some laughs and always remember - the magic within me is blessed to be witnessed by the magic within you!
Thanks for being here :D
— Nikki Cosma ＜3

From politics, history, religion, business, society + culture, to conversations about cults, conspiracy theories, and the forces shaping our lives. First, Read - new episodes every Wednesday. All Things Possible with Bethany Hamilton
Pro surfer, best-selling author, and Soul Surfer Bethany Hamilton joins her husband Adam Dirks to explore how faith, family, and intentional living make all things possible—even in life’s toughest moments. From raising kids and homeschooling to health, discipleship, and navigating today’s culture, this podcast delivers real conversations and practical encouragement. With inspiring guests and powerful stories, All Things Possible is your weekly dose of hope, resilience, and purpose-filled living.
Perfect for families of faith, homeschoolers, and anyone seeking joy and strength in the unexpected.

description:
The Spirit shares real stories of faith from people living in country Australia. It is for those already living with faith and those curious to know more about what that actually looks like. It is a glimpse into the lives of people living in country Australia and how being there has shaped their faith.

These are the stories of a police officer, an army cadet, a recipient of an Order of Australia and an advocate against family and domestic violence just to name a few. Their stories are not always neat, and each challenges us to think differently. But ultimately these are encouraging stories of hope.

The Spirit was recorded on the lands of the Dja Dja Wurrung, Baraba Baraba, Yorta Yorta and Bangerang peoples.

title: Disability Dialogues
author: Kimberly Walljasper
description:
Have you ever found yourself in a situation where you don’t know what to do or say because you don’t have any background knowledge or prior experiences?
Welcome to Disability Dialogues! This is a bi-weekly podcast that spreads awareness about individuals with disabilities. I am your host Kimberly Walljasper, and together we will explore inclusion through expert interviews as well as personal stories. Whether you have experience with individuals with disabilities or not, this podcast is here to provide you with resources so that you can be educated in this area and build meaningful connections.  
As a pre-service special education teacher with over eight years of experience working with various nonprofits like the Penguin Project, Spectrum Project, and Best Buddies, I’ve had the privilege of learning firsthand about the diverse challenges and triumphs faced by individuals with disabilities. From navigating the early days of volunteering to becoming deeply involved in advocacy, I’ve gained valuable insights into inclusion, adaptability, and understanding. This journey has taught me the importance of patience, perseverance, and building genuine connections. I understand the concerns you may have because I’ve walked that path myself, learning and growing alongside the incredible individuals I’ve had the honor to support.
Through my experience with individuals with disabilities, I discovered the incredible capabilities within every person, regardless of their challenges. This realization changed how I view inclusion, teaching me that everyone has a unique voice and story that deserves to be heard. This led me to start my podcast, where I can spotlight those stories and amplify voices often overlooked. Now, I want to share these powerful insights, personal triumphs, and practical guidance with you, helping to foster understanding and a more inclusive world for all.
So, if you’re ready to make a change in the community, subscribe to Disability Dialogues.

Health begins with communication. Every cell in the body relies on signals that support recovery, resilience, vitality, and healthy aging, yet cellular communication remains one of the most overlooked aspects of wellness.
Hosted by Russell Mariani and Mark Porteous, The Redox Podcast explores the science of redox signaling and why it matters through conversations with scientists, clinicians, educators, entrepreneurs, and wellness leaders. Complex science is translated into practical, accessible conversations that connect cellular health with everyday life, helping make sense of how the body communicates, adapts, and supports its own natural processes.
The ASEA Redox technology is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease or medical condition. The statements made during this podcast have not been evaluated by the Food and Drug Administration. People featured in this podcast may have received remuneration through the ASEA compensation plan. Most consumers of the ASEA redox products use them as part of an already healthy lifestyle. Results may vary. This information is intended for the US market only.

title: The Frozen River: A GMA Book Club Pick: A Novel by Ariel Lawhon
author: Momentum Media Advertising
description: Step into the biting winter of 1789 Maine to follow Martha Ballard, a daring midwife who defied a corrupt legal system to solve a shocking murder frozen in time. Through this gripping eight-part journey, you will transform from a passive observer of history into an active investigator of a forgotten past, unearthing the dangerous secrets of a town built on silence. By the season's end, you will see the early American frontier not through the eyes of its powerful men, but through the lens of a revolutionary woman who refused to be silenced.  Listeners will walk away with a profound framework for decoding historical bias, learning how to read between the lines of archival diaries to uncover marginalized voices. You will master the fundamentals of 18th-century forensic medicine, discovering how early midwives used empirical observation to challenge the formal authority of male physicians. Finally, you will gain a deep understanding of the structural legal barriers faced by colonial women and the exact strategies Martha used to navigate them.  This podcast is custom-made for historical fiction lovers, true crime addicts, and feminist history buffs who crave deep, character-driven mysteries based on real events. If you are someone who questions the official narrative and believes that the most important historical figures are the ones left out of the textbooks, this season is your sanctuary. Prepare to get lost in a world where a simple diary becomes the most dangerous weapon in the fight for justice.

title: Beyond the DX, Con Corazon Fuerte Podcast
author: Como La Flor
description:
Beyond the Dx: Con Corazón Fuerte

Mental health isn't just a diagnosis—it's a person, a family, a story, and a life.

I'm a medical assistant working in psychiatry, and after more than a year and a half in this field, I've come to realize that some of the most meaningful conversations don't happen during appointments—they happen in the quiet moments. The conversations with coworkers after a long day. The questions about medication side effects. The stories of trauma, resilience, heartbreak, hope, and healing. The things people carry that often go unseen.

Beyond the Dx: Con Corazón Fuerte was created to make space for those conversations.

This podcast is about looking beyond the diagnosis and remembering the human being behind it. We'll talk honestly about mental health, medication, stigma, trauma, relationships, culture, grief, recovery, and the everyday realities that don't always fit into a medical chart. We'll also explore how our experiences at work, at home, and throughout life shape our mental well-being.

One message that is especially close to my heart is this: asking for help is a sign of courage, not weakness. That message is for everyone, but especially for men who have often been taught to suffer in silence. You deserve support. You deserve to be heard.

We'll also have open conversations about psychiatric medications—what it's like to take them, common experiences, fears, and misconceptions. There is no shame in taking medication if it is part of your treatment plan. Seeking care for your mental health deserves the same compassion as seeking care for your physical health.

This podcast isn't about having all the answers. It's about creating a safe place for honest conversations, curiosity, compassion, and connection. It's about being vulnerable, learning together, and reminding each other that none of us have to navigate life's hardest moments alone.

While I work in psychiatry, this podcast reflects my personal views and experiences and is intended for educational and informational purposes only. It is not medical advice and should never replace care from your own healthcare professional. Any stories shared will respect patient privacy and confidentiality.

Thank you for being here. Whether you're living with a mental health condition, supporting someone you love, working in healthcare, or simply trying to understand yourself a little better, you are welcome here.

Let's go beyond the diagnosis—with compassion, honesty, and corazón fuerte.

title: A Loving Disruption with Elena Kunkel
author: Elena Kunkel
description:
A Loving Disruption starts with a claim I can’t leave alone: the opposite of depression isn’t happiness. It’s expression. 

By expression, I mean having access to all of yourself - your appetite, anger, humor, ideas, desire, taste, conviction - and letting those things have consequences. They change what you make, what you say, what you choose, what you refuse, how you love, and what you contribute.

I’m interested in the distance between functioning and feeling fully alive. How can someone be wildly capable and still feel like she’s managing her life from behind glass? What cracks it wide open? Why can making something honest bring you back into your body? Why do we become vague around the things we mean most? And what happens when fear has been holding the pen for so long that we mistake its voice for our own?

Each week, I follow those questions with people who have spent their lives approaching them from different directions: artists, founders, neuroscientists, somatic practitioners, coaches, philosophers, astrologers, Human Design people, and others who know something about what brings a person online. And what shuts her down.

They have different language for the same strange gap. I want to hear where their answers touch. Where they contradict each other. What each one can see that the others can’t.

This is the experiment: externally processing and testing the idea messily here, in my own life and with the sharpest people I can get in the room, until the language becomes specific enough to reach you when you feel far from yourself—and useful enough to help you find your way back.

title: Confidently Crypto (and More)

Whether you're crypto-curious, skeptical, or ready to take the plunge, this podcast is your go-to resource to navigate the exciting world of digital finance—and more—with confidence!
Dr. Evans, an attorney and former law professor, has testified before Congress, advises central bank crypto policy in West Africa, and she sits on the board of the Digital Currency Group. She has been in the rooms where it happens. She doesn’t hand you “crypto bro” hype. She delivers clarity and then gives you the tools to handle what will come next.

title: All Quiet on the Western Front: A Soldier's Story of World War I
author: Erich Maria Remarque
description: Paul Bäumer joins the German army with dreams of heroism, but the brutal reality of World War I shatters his illusions. As he navigates the treacherous trenches, Paul faces not only the enemy's bullets but also the devastating toll war takes on his body and mind. In 'All Quiet on the Western Front,' Erich Maria Remarque delivers a raw, unflinching look at the horrors of combat, exploring the deep emotional scars that linger long after the battle has ended. Through Paul's eyes, listeners witness the camaraderie of fellow soldiers, the stark contrast between the romanticized ideals of warfare and the grim truth of survival. This audiobook immerses you in the harrowing experiences that define a generation, revealing the haunting effects of war that ripple through every aspect of life. As Paul grapples with loss, disillusionment, and the struggle to find meaning in a shattered world, the central conflict of identity versus duty emerges. Will he ever reclaim the boy he once was? Join Paul on a journey that challenges the very essence of humanity, prompting listeners to reflect on the cost of conflict and the resilience of the human spirit. 'All Quiet on the Western Front' is not just a war story; it's a poignant exploration of what it means to endure and survive against all odds, making it a must-listen for anyone seeking a deeper understanding of the true impact of war.

title: The Customer Experience (CX)
author: Robert Zinsser
description:
The Consumer Has Many Choices

I do not need to tell any of you that are in Customer Service or trying to take care of your customers, no matter what your business involves, the last few years have been a challenge. Based on my involvement with many CX Professionals and the content being shared, customers are expecting a better experience and demanding some different ways to engage with your company.   Whether you are a convenience store or an insurance company, the CX is more important than it has ever been. How have you and your company adapted to delivering a great Customer Experience based on the new business requirements that have been forced upon us? The customer is still angry and stressed out so by going above and beyond, regardless of how they show up, may change their attitude. The little things matter, and you need to pay attention to every detail.   Picking the telephone up after 2 rings, responding to emails immediately or within a reasonable period of time (1 hour?), doing what you said you would do, being enthused about the person that is speaking with you, etc…..  I can’t stress enough about the importance of delivering a “great” Customer Experience (CX).   If you would like to discuss this topic further and the research that I have been following for over three years, send me an email to robert@getinthegamese.com or call me at 904.472.3706.

title: The ManFlu | Challenging Gender Roles, Marriage, and How We Parent Boys & Girls
author: Lucy McAfee
description:
You’ve heard of the “Manflu” — that mysterious illness that somehow hits men way harder than any woman could ever fathom. Well, we’re not handing out tissues, we’re here to talk about the real stuff underneath it: the disconnection, the imbalance, and how we can start to change it.


“The ManFlu” is a metaphor we are using to dig into something deeper: the cultural scripts, gender roles, and unspoken rules that keep men sidelined and leave women carrying more than their share of the mental and emotional load.


Host Lucy McAfee has spent years studying the impact of gender dynamics in our homes. As a wife and boy mom, she brings both research and lived experience to conversations about why these imbalances exist, how they affect families, and what we can do to build a more connected, balanced future for the next generation. Each week, The ManFlu combines insights and perspectives — featuring researchers, thinkers, and families themselves — to explore emotional labor, modern relationships, and raising evolved children. You’ll leave each episode not just nodding along but equipped with one small shift you can try at home.


Join us to lighten the load and build a future where women and men can both show up as their whole selves. New episodes drop every Wednesday — follow the show so you don’t miss one. And share it with your friends, because no one should have to face the Manflu epidemic alone.  


Follow Lucy on Instagram @lucypmcafee and @themanflupodcast for more on gender dynamics, mental and emotional load, and raising boys who care.

If you're a female coach or entrepreneur who's tired of playing small, tired of sounding like everyone else, and ready to build a brand — and a business — that's undeniably, magnetically you, this is your space.

title: Her Health Ally
author: Grace St Pierre
description:
Have you ever felt like you know something is wrong, but you just don't know what?

You feel on edge, maybe lingering symptoms bother you or someone you love. Fatigue, brain fog, low energy, forgetfulness...or maybe more physical symptoms such as heart racing, stomach upset, bloating, irregular cycles, hair or skin changes or pain. Or maybe its your child, with vague and persistent symptoms or behavior changes like irritability, melt-downs more often, waking up at night, or concerns like leg pain, headaches or belly pain. 

You FINALLY go to the doctor... (because let's face it, none of us want to deal with the 'brush off') and the labs come back "normal" and there's no plan. Or you get the "anxiety" talk from your provider... and offered band aid medications with no real plan and no follow up. 

You deserve to be heard. 

You deserve a Health Ally. 

Welcome, to Her Health Ally. I'm Grace, your hostess- an integrative family and lifestyle medicine nurse practitioner and mom of two. I've repeatedly seen the failings of the healthcare system- in myself, my children, my spouse, my family, my friends... I can't be silent. The best way I know to help- is to share, to educate, to empower you- to be the best advocate for yourself, your family you can be.  

You can ask questions without judgement. Your story is unique, it's yours alone. But you aren't alone any longer. I believe you. And now, I'm in your corner. 

No more isolation; no more feeling like you're the only one. Autoimmune disease, celiac, infertility, cancer, nutritional deficiencies, brain injuries, weight gain, early menopause, high blood pressure... I know the weight of the diagnoses.

I know what western medicine says, but I also know that there's more. Functional medicine, spiritual practices, acupuncture, acupressure, old wives tales with no studies to back them up because there's no profit in a simple cure.... There's more we can learn, more testing available that helps us understand, helps us know how to heal and be whole. To help our families be whole; body, mind and spirit. 

At Her Health Ally, you'll find hope, community, and direction. 

So, find and refill your water bottle, or warm up your coffee, and let's chat. Hope, community, direction. Let's go.

description: The Power of Being R.E.L.E.N.T.L.E.S.S: No Excuses What if every excuse you've ever made was quietly stealing your future? Rooted in scripture and driven by real-world wisdom, this episode lays the foundation for a transformative journey from passive thinking to relentless action. You'll discover why setbacks are a divine classroom, not a dead end, and how rising after every fall, not avoiding, it defines true resilience. Drawing on James 1:22, Proverbs 24:16, Romans 5:3–4, and more, this episode challenges you to stop waiting and start owning your outcomes. Accountability. Faith. Relentlessness. Your transformation starts here.

Each episode blends geology, geography, climatology, oceanography, and exploration into cinematic storytelling that reveals the extraordinary planet beneath our feet.

title: TRUCKHEADS
author: Michael Vincent
description:
Detention pay has been broken for thirty years. Everyone agrees it is broken. Nobody with the power to fix it has ever sat in that parking lot. That is not an accident. That is an industry that decided inconvenient truths are someone else's problem, as long as the panel discussing them has good lighting.

That is the gap TRUCKHEADS lives in.

Not a whitepaper. Not a keynote from someone who has never dispatched a truck but has strong opinions about efficiency. Just two guys, a microphone, and very little patience for legacy thinking that survives because fixing it would be inconvenient for whoever profits off the mess. Ignorance has been dressing up as expertise in this business for decades, getting promoted because it wears a tie and says "it's complicated" with a straight face. It is not complicated. It is just easier to leave alone.

Proverbs 27:17 says iron sharpens iron. Proverbs 12:1 says whoever hates correction is stupid. Read those together and you have the show's mission statement. This is not a roast. It is a correction, from people who love this industry too much to keep pretending it is fine.

The Dude and Captain Jav-tastic host it. They are not the point. The point is common sense finally said out loud instead of dying quietly in a comment section.

Find TRUCKHEADS on YouTube and everywhere you get podcasts.

Built for Truckheads. Finally, somebody said it.

Peace and Love.

title: Queering Compliance
author: Jennifer Gaskin
description:
Every queer person has a story. So does every compliance professional. Here, those two stories meet. Hosted by Jennifer Gaskin, editorial director of Corporate Compliance Insights, this podcast sits down with out LGBTQIA+ people working in compliance, ethics, risk, legal, governance and related fields to talk about the personal and professional journeys that brought them here — the challenges, the triumphs, the funny moments and the ones that still sting years or even decades later.
In a post-DEI world that would have queer people head back to the closet and stay there, there's never been a more important time to tell these stories. The arts, education, even sports — queer voices and queer stories are commonplace. In the corporate world — well, not so much. Queering Compliance is here to change that. Because joy is medicine. And so is being seen.
About the Host
Jennifer L. Gaskin is Editorial Director of Corporate Compliance Insights, joining the CCI team in 2021. In her professional role, she covers the intersection of corporate culture, ethics and the people who navigate both. She is also an out lesbian who watched the corporate DEI tide roll in and is watching it roll back out again. Before landing at CCI, Jennifer spent more than a decade in community journalism, serving in a variety of roles, from sports reporter to managing editor.
Read Jennifer's writing at CCI: https://www.corporatecomplianceinsights.com/author/jennifer-gaskin/
Visit her on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/jengaskin/
Interested in being a guest on the show?
Drop us a line: podcasts@corporatecomplianceinsights.com

title: The Path Beyond Binge Eating
author: Molly Dyer | Licensed Therapist, Certified Eating Disorder Specialist, Binge Eating Recovery Coach
description:
Binge eating has a way of making you feel like the problem — like if you just had more discipline, more willpower, more resolve, you'd finally get this under control. But binge eating isn't a willpower problem, and you were never the problem to begin with.


The Path Beyond Binge Eating is for women 30 and older who are ready to identify, interrupt, and replace binge eating, restriction, chronic dieting, and emotional eating — and start creating a relationship with food and body something that actually lasts.


Each week, Licensed Therapist, Certified Eating Disorder Specialist, and Binge Eating Recovery Coach Molly Dyer will invite you on a path through the practical steps of moving beyond binge eating and toward a consistent, sustainable relationship with food, your body, and yourself. 


Informed by 12 years of clinical experience and a Polyvagal lens, this show is not therapy — it is informational and educational, for women who are done waiting to lose weight or change their body to start living. This is where binge eating stops running the show and you start finding your way back to yourself.


New episodes every Wednesday. Follow the show and learn more about Molly, and how you can find support at www.MollyDyer.com

Whether you’re a seasoned clinician, a student, or simply passionate about mental health, we can't wait to share these conversations with you.
*Disclaimer:* This podcast is for educational and informational purposes only. It is not a substitute for therapy, clinical supervision, consultation, medical advice, or other professional services. We are not your therapists, supervisors or consultants and listening to this podcast does not establish a therapeutic, supervisory, or consultative relationship. Always use your own professional judgment and seek appropriate support for your specific clinical or personal needs.
